messages.text:
FIX
[src/user/gameplay/actor.hpp:14] — position is uninitialized

Why: Actor has no constructor and no default member initializer for position. If Vec3 is a plain struct, default-initialization leaves garbage. Init() copies this into base_position_, causing the actor to bob around an arbitrary world point.
Fix: Zero-initialize in the declaration: Vec3 position{}; or add Actor() : position{0, 0, 0} {}
Verify: Host smoke test constructs an Actor and asserts a.position.x == 0.0f && a.position.y == 0.0f && a.position.z == 0.0f
Strength: 1/2
Scope: local
Source: logic
[src/user/gameplay/spring_actor.hpp] — SpringActor missing IsCollectible() override

Why: SpringActor sets pickup_radius = 1.5f and overrides OnCollect(), clearly intending to be player-interactable. Yet it inherits Actor::IsCollectible() which returns false. When the gameplay scene eventually filters collision candidates via IsCollectible(), springs will be silently skipped.
Fix: Add bool IsCollectible() const override { return true; } inside the SpringActor class body.
Verify: Host smoke test constructs a SpringActor, calls Init(), and asserts actor.IsCollectible() == true
Strength: 1/2
Scope: local
Source: logic
[src/user/gameplay/refill_actor.hpp:13] — Redundant used state field

Why: used is perfectly correlated with the inherited collected field. OnCollect() sets both to true; respawn resets both to false. Two booleans for one state risk desynchronization and clutter the public API.
Fix: Remove used member; use collected instead in Update() and OnCollect().
Verify: g++ -std=c++17 -Isrc/user/gameplay -c src/user/gameplay/refill_actor.cpp compiles without errors.
Strength: 1/2
Scope: local
Source: simplification
[src/user/gameplay/strawberry_actor.cpp:15] — Redundant active = false in Update()

Why: OnCollect() already sets active = false immediately when collection happens. Repeating the assignment every frame in Update() is unnecessary and implies active and collected can diverge, which they shouldn't.
Fix: Remove active = false from Update(); just return when collected.
Verify: g++ -std=c++17 -Isrc/user/gameplay -c src/user/gameplay/strawberry_actor.cpp compiles without errors.
Strength: 1/2
Scope: local
Source: simplification
[src/user/gameplay/spring_actor.cpp:10] — Empty override of empty base method

Why: SpringActor::OnCollect() is identical to the base class empty implementation. It adds no behavior and requires a comment to explain why it does nothing.
Fix: Remove the override entirely.
Verify: g++ -std=c++17 -Isrc/user/gameplay -c src/user/gameplay/spring_actor.cpp compiles without errors.
Strength: 1/2
Scope: local
Source: simplification
[src/user/gameplay/strawberry_actor.hpp:16] / [src/user/gameplay/refill_actor.hpp:18] — Duplicated bobbing state and logic

Why: Both classes declare bob_time_ and base_position_, initialize them identically in Init(), and run identical sine bobbing in Update(). Any tuning change must be edited in two places.
Fix: Extract a BobbingActor base class with InitBobbing() and UpdateBobbing(float delta_seconds).
Verify: make produces madeline_cube_rom.z64 without compilation errors.
Strength: 1/2
Scope: cross-cutting
Source: simplification
NOTE
[src/user/gameplay/refill_actor.cpp:10] — Update() only returns early when used && !active. If external code ever sets active = false while used = false (e.g. to temporarily hide the actor), the refill continues to update its position.y bobbing even though it is inactive.
[src/user/gameplay/strawberry_actor.cpp:9] — Same pattern: Update() gates on collected but not on active. An externally-deactivated strawberry keeps bobbing.
[src/user/gameplay/refill_actor.cpp:26] — OnCollect() guards against used but not against active. If the gameplay scene (or a bug) calls OnCollect() on an inactive but unused refill, collection is still processed.
[src/user/gameplay/strawberry_actor.cpp:18] — OnCollect() does not guard against active or collected. Double-collection is harmless today, but if score-increment logic is added here later it will fire twice.
[src/user/gameplay/refill_actor.cpp:14] — After respawn, respawn_timer is left at its negative undershoot value instead of being reset to 0.0f. Harmless because OnCollect() reassigns it on next use, but any external reader of respawn_timer sees a stale negative value.
[src/user/gameplay/actor.hpp:15] — OnCollect() naming mismatch: SpringActor is not collectible (IsCollectible() == false) yet it has a pickup_radius and is touched by the player. The base class conflates "collectible" with "interactable"; if springs share the same touch logic, consider renaming OnCollect() to OnInteract() and pickup_radius to interaction_radius in a future refactor.

[Context file: /media/smk/Shared/Code/Celeste 64 Demake/AGENTS.md]
I'll  read  the  relevant  files  and  then  apply  the  fixes .
